applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to Conv3d layer, which improved performance from ~5.4ms to ~3.9ms by fusing operations and optimizing kernel launch

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with mode=max-autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: MLIR compilation errors with async_copy_global_to_local ops on ROCm
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to legalize operation 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' that was explicitly marked illegal

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton 3D convolution kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues with flattened grid indexing in Triton - most output elements mismatched
attempted_but_failed.error: Correctness issues - suspect Triton integer division/modulo behavior differs from Python for large program IDs

attempted_but_failed.name: FP16 tensor cores
attempted_but_failed.reason: Type mismatch between FP16 input and FP32 weights
attempted_but_failed.error: Input type (torch.cuda.HalfTensor) and weight type (torch.cuda.FloatTensor) should be the same

top_bottlenecks: 3D convolution GEMM operations highly optimized by MIOpen/rocBLAS

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') works correctly on ROCm and provides good optimization
torch.compile(mode='max-autotune') fails with MLIR async op errors
Custom Triton conv3d kernel had persistent correctness issues
3D convolution is dominated by GEMM operations that are extremely well optimized in MIOpen/rocBLAS - very difficult to beat with custom kernels
